items.html: *Clarification of the second discernment: it was at the time of the first Ibur [conception] of Zeir Anpin, when he was conceived in the innards of Ima, so as to be corrected — to join his six points in their discernments and incorporate them in one another, so that they would be a single authority [Reshut HaYachid], and not a public authority [Reshut HaRabim], separated from one another as at first. Therefore they were made there, through the Ibur, in a discernment of three incorporated in three: the six points in him were incorporated in one another, the three points incorporated in the three others, for HGT [Hesed-Gevura-Tifferet] were clothed within his NHY [Netzach-Hod-Yesod] — Hesed within Netzach, Gevura within Hod, and Tifferet within Yesod. And then the point of the Atara [crown] had no partner in which to be incorporated, after the likeness of the three mentioned; so the point of Malchut, which is the Atara, rose and clothed the Yesod. Thus, in the middle line there are three discernments, one within the other, while in the two other lines there are only two points, one within the other, in each and every line. It follows that the thickness of the middle line exceeds that of the other lines.
